note: First eval-harness release. Held-out: MSF3 (1054 paths) + CredData (1500 records).
9 new credential-format extractors + OOXML traversal + eval-gate CI workflow. Harness numbers unchanged by design (MSF3 has no content; CredData lacks new cred types).
4 new structured parsers (wp-config.php, AWS CLI creds, .netrc, Maven settings.xml) + harness history tracking + artifact upload. Same dynamic — new parsers don't fire on held-out sets.
4 more structured parsers (.pypirc, gcloud user creds, gh CLI hosts.yml, Python keyring) + harness trajectory visualisation + CI gate YAML fix. Parser count 22 → 26.

4 read-only HTTP verifiers (Stripe, SendGrid, Mailgun, Twilio) + PuTTY .ppk parser. 4th held-out set explicitly deferred — disk audit showed candidates are single-class or overfit-risk; faking one would defeat the discipline.

Acquired 3rd primary held-out set: Metasploitable 2 (Linux server filesystem, 1500 records, 34 positives labeled from public walkthroughs). MSF2 alone hits recall 0.971 + top-10 0.80 — confirms Linux server perf. MIN stays 0.20 (MSF3-bottlenecked, Windows). 4th set acquired through docker pull tleemcjr/metasploitable2 — no faking.

Falsified-hypothesis release. Tried adding score/sqrt(extension_frequency) penalty by analogy to v0.22 filename penalty; harness rejected (MSF3 0.20→0.10, MSF2 0.80→0.40). Backed out — discipline working. The 'minority extension = credential' assumption was Windows + dev shaped, not universal — Linux credential files live in .conf/.cnf/.php.

Added 4th held-out set: DiskForge synthetic Windows 10 share (Stauffer's tool, 12 plants at Snaffler/MITRE-documented credential paths, 43 records). Supplementary because 28% positive rate isn't realistic. Recall 0.917 (11/12 plants); top-10 0.50. Missed plant: .pypirc — parser exists but no rule for the filename, so cascade stays None. Documented gap for v0.30.

Closed the parser-without-rule gap surfaced by v0.29 DiskForge. 8 new rules in extra_rules.json for v0.24-v0.26 parser families (.pypirc, .netrc, gcloud creds, keyring, .aws/credentials, .m2/settings.xml, .config/gh/hosts.yml, .ppk). Engine 120→128 rules. DiskForge recall 0.917→1.00 (caught the missed .pypirc); top-10 0.50→0.60. MSF3/MSF2/CredData unchanged — new rules are filename-distinctive enough not to FP on Linux server filesystems. The loop working as designed.

Azure storage verifier (Shared Key HMAC-SHA256) — completes v0.23 extractor->verifier loop. GCP service-account verifier deferred: extractor catches only client_email, OAuth JWT exchange needs the private_key. DiskForge image grown to 519 records via 476 decoy negatives; positive density now 2.3% (was 28%), comparable to MSF3 + MSF2. Recall holds 1.00, top-10 0.60. Tests: 839 passing (+6 Azure).

Closed half of the v0.31 GCP gap. Added gcp_service_account_json extractor (multi-field regex capturing full {...} block, not just client_email) + GcpServiceAccountVerifier (structural validation: required fields, PEM-shaped private_key, well-formed email). Verifier coverage: 19 → 20. Live OAuth verification (JWT signing + token exchange) deferred to v0.33+ — would add pyjwt as opt-in dep. Tests: 849 passing (+10 GCP). Harness unchanged.

Closed the second half of the v0.31 GCP gap. GCP verifier now signs RS256 JWT with the captured private_key and exchanges at oauth2.googleapis.com/token; 200 → validation_mode=live, 401/400 → failed with HTTP status, timeout/connection error → inconclusive. Graceful fallback to structural verdict when pyjwt[crypto] isn't installed. pyjwt[crypto]>=2.0 added to verify group. Tests: 857 passing (+8 live OAuth tests, all HTTP mocked, synthetic 2048-bit RSA key generated at test time).

DiskForge GCP SA JSON plant + end-to-end integration tests. Synthetic 2048-bit RSA key + valid SA JSON shape, planted at /Users/Administrator/Documents/gcp_service_account.json. DiskForge: 13 plants (was 12); recall stays 1.000; top-10 0.60. 4 integration tests confirm extractor catches the planted JSON, structural verifier passes, live verifier signs real JWT and accepts mocked OAuth 200. Tests: 861 passing (+4).

MIN top-10 jumps 0.2 → 0.7 — first movement in 16+ releases. Root cause was the v0.21 Green-tier lesson never fully wiring through the ranking logic. The v0.22 _TIER_PSEUDO_P[Green]=0.0 was correct but the max(path_probability, cascade_tier_pseudo_p) reduce let path_probability=1.0 override cascade_tier=Green. v0.44 step 2 short-circuits to evidence=0 when cascade_tier=Green explicitly. Per-set: MSF3 0.2 → 0.8 (Boxstarter Relay-matched .ps1 flood removed), engagement_corpus 0.4 → 0.9, MSF2 stays 1.0, CredData stays 0.7, DiskForge stays 0.5. Recall preserved at 0.9 (no credentials sacrificed). Production CLI also gets the fix — operators running ``sharesift score-paths`` see real credentials in top-10 now (e.g. id_rsa, authorized_keys) instead of installer-script noise. Tests: 1274 passing (+20 ranking, 0 regressions).
